reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): Passed - streamBlobBatch is actively used by blobSidecarsByRangeRPCHandler (L107). Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): Passed - attack path is via P2P network (untrusted peers). Gate 3 (Code Verification): Passed - code reading is factually correct. streamBlobBatch at rpc_blob_sidecars_by_range.go:21-58 iterates over batch.canonical() and serves all blobs using MaxBlobsForEpoch() for iteration count only, with no epoch-based filtering logic. No check exists for compute_epoch(sidecar.slot) < FULU_FORK_EPOCH. Gate 4 (Exploitability): Passed - attacker can trigger this by requesting blob_sidecars_by_range with a range spanning the fork boundary via P2P. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): Property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-029 from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json (line 787-803) states: 'During transition, blob_sidecars_by_range must serve only pre-Fulu epoch blobs.' Assertion: 'forall sidecar in transition_resp: compute_epoch(sidecar.slot) < FULU_FORK_EPOCH'. The code does not implement this epoch filter. Gate 6 (Scope Check): Passed - this is a Fusaka-specific transition bug (in scope). Severity: Medium is appropriate - this is a protocol compliance bug affecting the deprecation transition, but impact is limited to serving incorrect data via a deprecated RPC method. The actual security impact depends on whether receiving post-Fulu blobs via blob_sidecars_by_range causes downstream issues in peer clients during the transition window.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: During transition, blob_sidecars_by_range must serve only pre-Fulu epoch blobs. (assertion: forall sidecar in transition_resp: compute_epoch(sidecar.slot) < FULU_FORK_EPOCH)
